question_text: A puddle of water gradually disappears on a warm sunny day. What is the name of this process and what happens to the water molecules?
correct_answer: The process is evaporation. Water molecules at the surface gain enough energy to escape into the air as water vapour.
explanation: Evaporation occurs when particles at the surface of a liquid gain enough thermal energy to overcome attractive forces and escape into the gas phase. This happens at temperatures below the boiling point.
